content.problemStatement:
Concatenation of two integers is obtained as follows: First, convert both integers to strings. Then concatenate both strings into one and convert this concatenated string back to integer.

For example, concatenation of 666 and 777 is CONC(6,7)=67CONC(6, 7) = 67CONC(6,7)=67, concatenation of 123123123 and 454545 is CONC(123,45)=12345CONC(123, 45) = 12345CONC(123,45)=12345.

You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers. You are also given two integers LLL and RRR. Find the number of pairs (iii, jjj) such that  1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N1 \leq i, j \leq N1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N and   L‚Č§CONC(Ai,Aj)‚Č§RL \leq CONC(A_i, A_j) ‚Č§ RL‚Č§CONC(Ai‚Äč,Aj‚Äč)‚Č§R

**Note:** Since the size of the input and output is large, please use fast input-output methods.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains three integers N,L,RN, L, RN,L,R.
The second line of each test case line contains NNN integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2,\dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line the number of suitable pairs.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1:


(i=1,j=1)(i = 1, j = 1)(i=1,j=1): CONC(A1,A1)=22CONC(A_1, A_1) = 22CONC(A1‚Äč,A1‚Äč)=22 and 10‚Č§22‚Č§5210 \leq 22 \leq 5210‚Č§22‚Č§52.


(i=1,j=2)(i = 1, j = 2)(i=1,j=2): CONC(A1,A2)=25CONC(A_1, A_2) = 25CONC(A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč)=25 and 10‚Č§25‚Č§5210 \leq 25 \leq 5210‚Č§25‚Č§52.


(i=2,j=1)(i = 2, j = 1)(i=2,j=1): CONC(A2,A1)=52CONC(A_2, A_1) = 52CONC(A2‚Äč,A1‚Äč)=52 and 10‚Č§52‚Č§5210 \leq 52 \leq 5210‚Č§52‚Č§52.


(i=2,j=2)(i = 2, j = 2)(i=2,j=2): CONC(A2,A2)=55CONC(A_2, A_2) = 55CONC(A2‚Äč,A2‚Äč)=55 and 10‚Č§5510 \leq 5510‚Č§55 but 55‚Čį52 55 \nleq 5255‚Čį52.


So there are three suitable pairs.
Test case 2: There is no suitable pair.
Test case 3: The suitable pairs are (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1) and (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2).
